extraction.one_line_summary: MODOLO Sacha won stage 3 of Tour de Luxembourg after breakaway containing virtual leader O'CONNOR Ben was caught inside final kilometers despite half-hearted peloton chase
tactical_patterns: ALMEIDA João's DECEUNINCK QUICK-STEP chased O'CONNOR breakaway to protect 4-second GC deficit before final day TT, revealing willingness to work.
Sprinter teams split chase effort with GC squads when breakaway contains virtual leader, creating slower pursuit than pure sprint stage.
BAUER Jack bridge attempt with 12.2km remaining triggered coordinated peloton response, suggesting breakaway could have survived without late aggression.

rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: positive: positioned in breakaway to gain time as virtual leader, forcing GC teams into chase work
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: team chased O'CONNOR breakaway aggressively despite wearing points jersey, prioritizing GC before TT stage
rider_signals.MODOLO Sacha: positive: ended long winless drought in messy sprint, suggesting form timed for less-organized finales
